title: My Ask Is Aligned with My Value
subtitle: An energy statement for releasing the guilt that silences the raise conversation before it starts
excerpt: A short energy statement for the belief that asking for more is demanding or ungrateful. The release names the guilt driving the silence; the allow names a practice of asking clearly, from the full worth of the work.
sourceNotes: Original energy statement written for homemade.education. The release-and-allow framework is from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).

content.text: An energy statement for the silence around asking. The release names the guilt that reads a raise conversation as ingratitude or a demand. The allow names the other option: asking directly, from the value of the work, without the upfront apology.

I am ready to release the belief that asking for more makes me ungrateful or difficult, that my market rate is somehow too much to ask for, or that waiting to be noticed is the more considerate option. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.

I am ready to align with and allow the practice of asking clearly, from the full value of my work, without the pre-emptive softening that dilutes the ask before it is even received. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Say each three times, slow, out loud or in your mind.
Written for homemade.education. The release-and-allow structure is from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
